title: Project Init
description: Getting a specd project off the ground requires creating a config file, storage directories, and optionally installing agent skills -- doing this manually is tedious and error-prone. The `specd project init` command bootstraps a new specd project, presenting a guided wizard in interactive mode or proceeding silently with flags in non-interactive mode. All file creation is delegated to the `InitProject` use case in `@specd/core`.
context: Getting a specd project off the ground requires creating a config file, storage directories, and optionally installing agent skills -- doing this manually is tedious and error-prone. The `specd project init` command bootstraps a new specd project, presenting a guided wizard in interactive mode or proceeding silently with flags in non-interactive mode. All file creation is delegated to the `InitProject` use case in `@specd/core`.

rules:
```
specd project init [--schema <ref>] [--workspace <id>] [--workspace-path <path>] [--agent <id>...] [--force] [--format text|json|toon]
```

- `--schema <ref>` тАФ optional; the schema reference to activate (e.g. `@specd/schema-std`, `./schemas/custom/schema.yaml`). Defaults to `@specd/schema-std`.
- `--workspace <id>` тАФ optional; the ID of the default workspace. Defaults to `default`.
- `--workspace-path <path>` тАФ optional; the specs path for the default workspace, relative to the config file. Defaults to `specs/`.
- `--agent <id>` тАФ optional, repeatable; agents to install skills for immediately after initialisation (e.g. `--agent claude`). When not provided in interactive mode, the wizard asks.
- `--force` тАФ optional; overwrite an existing config without prompting.
- `--format text|json|toon` тАФ optional; forces non-interactive mode; defaults to `text`.

The command enters interactive mode when **all** of the following are true:

- stdout is a TTY
- `--format` is not `json` or `toon`
- No configuration flags (`--schema`, `--workspace`, `--workspace-path`, `--agent`) are provided

In interactive mode the command presents a guided wizard:

1. **Welcome header** тАФ an ASCII banner and a short description of specd, shown once at startup.
2. **Project settings prompts** тАФ prompts for each setting with its default value pre-filled:
   - Schema reference (default: `@specd/schema-std`)
   - Default workspace ID (default: `default`)
   - Specs path (default: `specs/`)
3. **Agent selection** тАФ a multi-select listing all known agents. The user selects which agents to install skills for. Selecting none is valid (skills can be installed later with `specd skills install`).
4. **Confirmation summary** тАФ shows the resolved settings and asks for confirmation before writing any files. If the user cancels, the command exits with code 0 and no files are written.
5. **Progress** тАФ a spinner is shown while `InitProject` runs and skills are installed.
6. **Completion** тАФ a success message with the project root path and next-step hints.

When not in interactive mode, the command uses the flag values (or defaults) directly, calls `InitProject`, optionally installs skills for agents specified via `--agent`, and prints output according to `--format`.

- `text`: prints `initialized specd in <absolute-project-root-path>` followed by any skills installed.
- `json` or `toon`: outputs `{"result":"ok","configPath":"...","schema":"...","workspaces":[...],"skillsInstalled":{}}` where `skillsInstalled` maps agent ids to arrays of installed skill names.

The target directory is the **git root** when inside a git repository, or the **current working directory** otherwise. This resolution happens in the CLI before entering the wizard or calling `InitProject`.

The CLI passes the resolved project root, schema reference, workspace id, workspace specs path, and force flag to `InitProject`. All file creation тАФ `specd.yaml`, storage directories, `.gitignore` entry тАФ is handled by `InitProject` via `ConfigWriter`. The CLI never touches `specd.yaml` directly.

After `InitProject` succeeds, if any agents were selected (interactively or via `--agent`), the CLI installs all skills for each selected agent using the same logic as `specd skills install all --agent <id>`, and records the installations in `specd.yaml` via `RecordSkillInstall`.

If `specd.yaml` already exists and `--force` is not provided:

- **Interactive mode**: the wizard informs the user and asks whether to overwrite or cancel.
- **Non-interactive mode**: exits with code 1 and prints an `error:` message to stderr.

constraints: specd project init does not require an existing specd.yaml to run; standard config discovery is not invoked
The CLI never reads or writes YAML тАФ all config format knowledge lives in @specd/core
--schema accepts any string; validation is deferred to first use
Interactive mode is never entered when --format json or --format toon is set, even if stdout is a TTY тАФ these formats imply scripted use

scenarios.name: Unknown schema ref does not error at init time
then: the command exits with code 0
AND specd.yaml is written with the given schema reference

scenarios.name: Interactive mode is skipped when --format json is set
when: specd project init --format json is run with no other flags
then: no wizard prompts are shown
AND the command runs non-interactively with defaults
AND stdout is valid JSON

scenarios.name: Interactive mode is skipped when any config flag is provided
then: no wizard prompts are shown

scenarios.name: Default values produce a valid minimal config
when: specd project init --workspace default --workspace-path specs/ is run
then: specd.yaml is written with schema: '@specd/schema-std', workspace default at specs/
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: JSON output contains all fields including skillsInstalled
when: specd project init --format json --agent claude is run
then: stdout is valid JSON with result, configPath, schema, workspaces, and skillsInstalled fields

scenarios.name: Config written to git root when inside a subdirectory
given: a git repository with root at /repo and the CWD is /repo/packages/foo
when: specd project init --workspace default is run
then: specd.yaml is written to /repo/specd.yaml

scenarios.name: Config written to CWD when not inside a git repository
given: a directory that is not inside any git repository
when: specd project init --workspace default is run
then: specd.yaml is written in the current working directory

scenarios.name: Standard directories are created
given: a project root with no pre-existing change directories
when: specd project init --workspace default completes successfully
then: changes/, drafts/, discarded/, and archive/ directories exist under the project root

scenarios.name: .gitignore entry is added
given: no .gitignore exists in the project root
when: specd project init --workspace default completes successfully
then: a .gitignore is created containing specd.local.yaml

scenarios.name: Skills are installed for selected agents after init
when: specd project init --workspace default --agent claude is run
then: skill files are written to <git-root>/.claude/commands/
AND specd.yaml records the installed skills under skills.claude

scenarios.name: Non-interactive mode fails without --force
given: a specd.yaml already exists at the target path
when: specd project init --workspace default is run
then: the command exits with code 1
AND stderr contains an error: message
AND the existing specd.yaml is unchanged

scenarios.name: --force overwrites existing config
when: specd project init --force --schema @specd/schema-std --workspace default is run
then: the command exits with code 0
AND specd.yaml now contains the new schema reference
